title: Add bundles pack and import commands to runex skill bundles.nu
description:
The runex skill's bundles.nu has list/show/validate but no pack or import commands. The Runex API has POST /api/bundles/import accepting multipart tar.gz uploads (BundleController.import_bundle at lib/runex_web/controllers/api/bundle_controller.ex:8). We need:

1. bundles.nu pack <bundle-dir> — creates a tar.gz archive from a bundle directory. Output to stdout path or --output flag. Uses 'tar -czf' under the hood.
2. bundles.nu import <archive-path> --host <runex-url> — POSTs the tar.gz to the target Runex's /api/bundles/import endpoint as multipart form upload. Returns the import result.

These are needed for vantageex-219 (pack VantageEx bundles and import to Mini's Runex) and for the bundle versioning epic (vantageex-218).

File: plugins/tools/runex/skills/runex/scripts/0.1.0/bundles.nu
close_reason: Fixed in PR #52
acceptance_criteria:
- bundles.nu pack creates a valid tar.gz from any bundle directory
- bundles.nu import uploads the tar.gz to a target Runex and returns the result
- Both commands have --help output
- Tested manually against localhost:4001

title: fix: /core:security hook uses relative path — check-secrets-before-commit.sh fails on every Bash call
description:
The /core:security skill's SKILL.md frontmatter defines a PreToolUse:Bash hook with a relative command path:

  command: "./hooks/check-secrets-before-commit.sh"

This resolves from the user's current working directory (e.g. ~/github/vantageex), not from the skill's own directory. The script only exists at the skill's hooks/ directory. Result: every Bash tool call produces 5+ non-blocking errors:

  PreToolUse:Bash hook error
  Failed with non-blocking status code: /bin/sh: ./hooks/check-secrets-before-commit.sh: No such file or directory

Fix: change the command to use the CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT variable (same pattern the security-guidance and hookify plugins use):

  command: "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/check-secrets-before-commit.sh"

File: plugins/core/skills/security/SKILL.md (frontmatter hooks section, around line 10)
Also verify the script exists at: plugins/core/skills/security/hooks/check-secrets-before-commit.sh

One-line fix. Bump the plugin version in plugin.json after.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #52
acceptance_criteria:
- Hook command uses absolute path via CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT
- Loading /core:security skill no longer produces hook errors on Bash calls
- The gitleaks scan still runs correctly when the hook fires (test by running a Bash command after loading the skill)

title: feat(github): add dependabot-consolidator skill — collect, consolidate, verify & close out Dependabot PRs
description:
## Objective
Ship a new github-plugin skill 'dependabot-consolidator' codifying the recipe proven on kina PR #36: collect a repo's open Dependabot PRs, consolidate into ONE branch+PR grouped by ecosystem/risk, verify with baseline-diff gates, then on operator approval squash-merge and close out (comment each superseded Dependabot PR as addressed, confirm closure). Single-repo, operator-supervised.

## Context
- Derived from real run: kina PR #36 (sha2 0.10->0.11, actions/checkout 4->6, actions/cache 4->5, action-gh-release 2.2.1->3.0.0).
- Home: plugins/tools/github/. Bump github plugin 0.1.3 -> 0.2.0.
- Opus design doc held in operator session; grounded vs test/validate-skills-quality.nu.

## CRITICAL
- Frontmatter name MUST equal dir 'dependabot-consolidator' (legacy github- prefix = hard name_dir failure).
- SKILL.md body cap <=500 lines (enforced); target <300 via references/.
- references/*.md must NOT contain the literal token 'references/' (depth check trips on substring).
- Every references/ + agents/ basename mentioned in SKILL.md (orphan check); every /plugin:skill token resolves.
- Must mention anti-fabrication; no allowed-tools field.
- Merge step is OPERATOR-APPROVED then skill-executed (consistent with agents-never-merge-unilaterally); skill never merges without the green-local + green-remote + operator-go gate.

## Deliverables
- New skill dir (SKILL.md + 5 references + 2 agents), 3 registration edits, one PR on a feature branch. User approves squash merge.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #99 (merged) — /github:dependabot-consolidator skill, github plugin v0.2.0
acceptance_criteria:
- SKILL.md created (name=dir, third-person desc + 'Use when', license MIT).
- 5 references: consolidation-algorithm, baseline-diff-verification, group-by-risk, local-integration-gates, merge-and-closeout.
- 2 agents: dependabot-collector (haiku, read-only gh list/classify) + dependabot-consolidator-worker (sonnet, cherry-pick + stop-and-report, never auto-merge).
- Encodes: baseline-diff gate (run check on main first; only NEW failure blocks), keep-every-bump conflict + pin-style preservation, lockfile git check-ignore, group-by-ecosystem/risk.
- Encodes CLOSE-OUT: after green-local+green-remote+operator-approval -> gh pr merge --squash (no attribution); comment each superseded Dependabot PR 'addressed by #<consolidated>'; verify each Dependabot PR closed via gh pr view (state MERGED/CLOSED) rather than assuming auto-close.
- Registration: marketplace.json github 0.2.0 + desc/keywords; plugin.json 0.2.0 + skills[] + new agents[]; sources.md Dependabot section.
- BOTH validators pass: nu test/validate-plugin.nu github AND nu test/validate-skills-quality.nu (no new non-baselined failures).

title: zig skill: add references/migration-0.16.md with before/after snippets for the 0.16 breakages we keep hitting
description:
## Objective
The zig SKILL.md states 0.16 migrations as terse one-liners ('sync primitives moved to std.Io.*'); agents quote it but still botch the specific APIs. Add references/migration-0.16.md with BEFORE→AFTER code snippets for each breakage observed in production: (1) std.Thread.Mutex → std.Io.Mutex (or a local SpinLock for test fakes); (2) std.io.fixedBufferStream/AnyReader → std.Io.Reader.fixed + *std.Io.Reader; (3) managed std.ArrayList .init/.append(x)/.deinit() → unmanaged .empty/.append(gpa,x)/.deinit(gpa); (4) std.StringHashMap (unordered) vs std.StringArrayHashMapUnmanaged (insertion order) — when order matters; (5) std.c.* (getenv/environ/setenv) requires .link_libc=true on the module + the extern fn pattern, and prefer std.crypto.random over std.c.arc4random_buf for portability; (6) std.process.run takes an io param in 0.16; (7) macOS 26 builds need DEVELOPER_DIR=/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools (cold-cache compiler_rt link). Link it one level deep from SKILL.md's version table.

## AC
- references/migration-0.16.md created with the 7 before/after blocks; SKILL.md links it; sources.md updated; validator green.

## Load skills
/claude-code:skill-update /claude-code:claude-skills /zig:zig
close_reason: merged: PR #98 (squash 14bd160) — references/migration-0.16.md, 7 before/after sections verified vs installed 0.16 stdlib

title: awman skill: document docker-sbx-experimental prerequisite (Docker account + `sbx login`)
description: The awman runtime reference should state that the docker-sbx-experimental runtime requires a Docker account and a one-time `sbx login` before `awman ready` succeeds with that runtime. Operator-confirmed prerequisite 2026-06-13. Keep generic (public repo).

title: agent-loop workflows-execution: harden five-tier gates (working-tree dirtiness + zig cache-poisoning gotchas)
description:
## Objective
Two doctrine updates for core/skills/agent-loop references/workflows-execution.md + templates/five-tier-issue.workflow.js, learned in production:
1. The diff-boundary gate must check BOTH `git diff <testSha>..HEAD -- <testFiles>` AND `git status --porcelain -- <testFiles>` (an implementer made frozen tests pass via UNCOMMITTED edits — committed-diff-only gates miss it), and must re-run after the fix loop, not only before it.
2. CI-runner stage must require a clean working tree before reporting green (dirty-tree green is an illusion).
Optional addendum for a zig reference: toolchain version-string cache poisoning — two different zig builds sharing one version string share global cache keys, so artifacts from a removed toolchain can keep a broken toolchain green until the cache goes cold; verify stdlib checksums against the official tarball, not version output.

## Context
Observed on a private project 2026-06-12 across two five-tier runs. Keep examples generic — public repo, no private refs.

## Load skills
/claude-code:claude-skills /core:agent-loop /core:tdd

title: fix sources-validate-urls.nu repo-root off-by-one
description: The repo-root function in plugins/tools/claude-code/skills/skill-update/scripts/sources-validate-urls.nu uses seven '..' joins from FILE_PWD, but the script lives at depth 6 from the repo root (plugins/tools/claude-code/skills/skill-update/scripts/). Seven '..' resolves to /Users/vinnie/github instead of /Users/vinnie/github/claude-skills, causing 'marketplace.json not found at /Users/vinnie/github/.claude-plugin/marketplace.json' when running 'mise sources:validate'. The same off-by-one bug is present in the four sibling scripts (sources-check.nu, sources-report.nu, sources-stale.nu all use identical 7-'..' chains). Fix is to change all repo-root definitions from 7 '..' to 6 '..' consistently across all five scripts in that directory.
close_reason: merged in PR #94 https://github.com/vinnie357/claude-skills/pull/94

title: core:container: 1.0.0 run flags wrong -- --stop-signal not a run option (signal lives on container stop); --shm-size verified present
description:
## Problem

The skill documents two flags as 1.0.0+ additions to `container run`:

1. `--stop-signal` — claimed as a `container run` option in multiple places
2. `--shm-size` — claimed as a `container run` option

Verified against container CLI 1.0.0 (commit ee848e3):
- `--stop-signal` does NOT exist on `container run` — `container run --help` has no such flag
- `--shm-size` IS present on `container run` (confirmed via `container run --help | grep shm`: shows `--shm-size <shm-size>  Size of /dev/shm (e.g. 64M, 1G)`)
- Signal control lives on `container stop` via `-s / --signal <signal>` (confirmed via `container stop --help`)

File:line locations of wrong claims:

- `plugins/core/skills/container/SKILL.md:187` — example uses `--stop-signal SIGTERM` on `container run`
- `plugins/core/skills/container/references/command-reference.md:53` — table row: `--stop-signal | | Signal sent to stop the container (1.0.0+)`
- `plugins/core/skills/container/references/version-history.md:29` — release note claims `--stop-signal on container run`
- `plugins/core/skills/container/templates/1.0.0/commands.md:21` — bullet: `--stop-signal option on container run`
- `plugins/core/skills/container/templates/1.0.0/commands.md:94` — table: `container run | --stop-signal and --shm-size options added`
- `plugins/core/skills/container/templates/1.0.0/commands.md:99` — example: `container run --stop-signal SIGTERM -d --name app myapp:latest`

## Evidence

CLI version:
  container CLI version 1.0.0 (build: release, commit: ee848e3)

`container run --help | grep -iE 'stop-signal|shm'`:
  --shm-size <shm-size>   Size of /dev/shm (e.g. 64M, 1G)
  (no --stop-signal line)

`container stop --help` (signal IS here):
  -s, --signal <signal>   Signal to send to the containers

## Fix

- `--stop-signal`: Remove from all `container run` examples and flag tables. The correct form for custom stop signals is `container stop --signal <SIG>` (or `-s <SIG>`). The SKILL.md example at line 187 and the templates/1.0.0/commands.md example at line 99 should be removed or replaced with a `container stop` example. The command-reference.md table row should move to the `container stop` flags section. The version-history.md entry should be corrected to say the signal flag is on `container stop`, not `container run`.
- `--shm-size`: No change needed — verified present on `container run` in 1.0.0.

## Affected files

- `plugins/core/skills/container/SKILL.md` line 187 (and ~190 for shm-size example — shm-size example is correct, stop-signal example is wrong)
- `plugins/core/skills/container/references/command-reference.md` line 53
- `plugins/core/skills/container/references/version-history.md` line 29
- `plugins/core/skills/container/templates/1.0.0/commands.md` lines 21, 94, 99

title: [task][P3] /core:nushell — document bash test/&&/|| silent error-mask gotcha
description:
## What

Add a "Common Pitfalls" subsection to the /core:nushell skill (and possibly /core:git-operations or a generic shell skill) documenting that bash `&& ... ||` patterns silently mask command failures.

## Reproduction

Pattern that bit us:
```bash
test -f /path/to/binary && codesign --force --sign - /path/to/binary || echo skip
```

Intent: codesign the binary if it exists; otherwise skip.

Reality: if codesign FAILS (binary already signed with immutable signature, permission denied, etc.), the `|| echo skip` fires and the WHOLE command exits 0. The script proceeds as if codesign succeeded, and downstream steps (start-service) run with an unsigned binary → macOS Tahoe SIGKILL → service won't start.

The Nushell guard `if $cs_burrito.exit_code != 0` never fires because the bash exit code is 0.

## Correct shape

Use bash `if/else`, not `&& ... ||`:
```bash
if test -f /path/to/binary; then
  codesign --force --sign - /path/to/binary
else
  echo "skip — binary absent"
fi
```

This propagates codesign's exit code correctly. Failures surface to the calling script.

## Why it matters

The `cmd1 && cmd2 || cmd3` shorthand is a CLASSIC bash footgun. It looks like "if cmd1 then cmd2 else cmd3" but it's actually "if (cmd1 && cmd2) succeeds then 0; else cmd3". When `cmd2` legitimately fails, `cmd3` runs and the OVERALL exit is 0 (assuming cmd3 succeeds). The "else" branch fires on ANY non-zero exit upstream, not just the missing-precondition case.

For non-zero-tolerant commands (codesign, ssh, scp, kubectl apply, mix release, etc.) this masks real failures.

## Suggested insertion

`/core:nushell` skill, "Common Pitfalls" section. Title: "Bash `&& ... ||` Silent Error Mask in Embedded Shell Commands". Body:

> When invoking bash from Nushell (e.g., `^bash -c "..."` or via mise's task runner), avoid `cmd1 && cmd2 || cmd3` for conditional execution. Use bash `if/else` instead so the inner command's exit code propagates correctly.
>
> WRONG (silent error mask):
> ```bash
> test -f path && do-thing || echo skip
> ```
> If `do-thing` fails, `echo skip` fires and the OVERALL command exits 0. Caller cannot detect the failure.
>
> CORRECT (propagates exit code):
> ```bash
> if test -f path; then do-thing; else echo skip; fi
> ```
>
> This bit us in deploy-to-mini's codesign step (PR #80 / runex-workflows): codesign failed silently, binary stayed unsigned, macOS 26 Tahoe SIGKILLed it on start.

## Reference

- runex-workflows PR #80 (the fix)
- feedback_macos26_codesign_on_deploy.md (related macOS gotcha; this is the deeper why-it-wasn't-caught)
- Bash POSIX spec on logical-operator precedence: `&&` and `||` left-associative, both at same precedence; `cmd1 && cmd2 || cmd3` parses as `(cmd1 && cmd2) || cmd3`

## Acceptance

- /core:nushell SKILL.md "Common Pitfalls" section gains the bash `&& ... ||` subsection with WRONG/CORRECT examples + reference to PR #80
- (Optional) cross-link from /core:git-operations or a generic shell-scripts skill if/when one exists
- Bumped /core:* plugin version per claude-skills versioning rules
close_reason: extracted to /core:nushell references/shell-interop.md (Bash logical-operator section) in PR #78 (squash 764d30b); 1 root source memory deleted

title: skill-extract: CI discipline + docs-first TDD rules → /core:tdd
description:
Extract from private memories into /core:tdd SKILL.md.

Rules:
- mise run ci must pass before EVERY commit (not just pre-push) — this is the TDD red-green-refactor gate
- CI green = local mise run ci + gh pr checks both passing — neither alone is sufficient
- Agents must paste verbatim CI output — 'I ran it' is insufficient evidence
- No pre-existing failure carve-outs — every failing test is the current agent's responsibility
- After merge: verify post-merge main CI separately (sequence merges, don't batch)
- Docs first: ADRs + design docs BEFORE implementation. TDD from documented specs.
- No fabricated time estimates without measurement.

Source memories: feedback_ci_discipline.md, feedback_docs_first_tdd.md, feedback_no_fabricated_time_estimates.md
close_reason: extracted to /core:tdd references/ci-discipline.md in PR #78 (squash 764d30b); 3 archive source memories deleted

title: skill-extract: infra rules (NGINX, Kustomize+Helm, IaC user classes) → /core:twelve-factor
description:
Extract from private memories into /core:twelve-factor or new infra skill.

Rules:
- NGINX Inc (nginx.org) only — never the community ingress controller
- Kustomize + Helm together for k8s (base + overlays, non-secret values only in repo)
- 1Password as universal secret source for all deployment targets
- IaC for every service — never manual UI. Two user classes: admins control IaC, general users author epics. Default to velocity; gate only secrets + data destruction.
- Services reachable over Tailscale MUST bind all interfaces (0.0.0.0) — never 127.0.0.1 in prod.

title: skill-extract: Linear epic body rules (no YAML, skill labels, plain markdown) → /linear:plan-epic
description:
Extract from private memories into /linear:plan-epic.

Rules:
- Linear epic bodies use plain markdown for Skills/Repos/Constraints sections — NOT YAML fences. VantageEx parser captures fence lines as garbage labels.
- Skills/Repos sections use plain bullet lists, not code blocks.
- Skill labels listed in epic body must exist in the claude-skills marketplace.
- Core skills (anti-fabrication, git, tdd, twelve-factor, security, mise, nushell) stay implicit and must NOT be listed in epic bodies.
- Initial state for new epics: Backlog. Labels are team-scoped; missing skill labels get auto-created on first use.

Source memories: feedback_no_yaml_in_epic_bodies.md
close_reason: extracted to /linear:linear SKILL.md (Epic Body Format Rules) + commands/plan-epic.md pointer in PR #78 (squash 764d30b); 1 archive source memory deleted

title: skill-extract: bees claude-teams-aware format + single-writer rule → /core:bees
description:
Extract from private memories into /core:bees SKILL.md. Note: claude-skills-32 already covers bees-manager serial-writer. This bee adds the structured-format rule.

Rules:
- Bees for agent pickup need team:*/skill:*/model:* labels plus structured description sections (CRITICAL / Objective / Context / Acceptance / Deliverables / Load skills). Single-paragraph bees are operator-only.
- SQLite single-writer constraint: workers MUST NOT run bees write commands (new/close/dep add). Collect BEES REQUESTS in worker final report; lead applies via serial bees-manager agent.
- bees ready order = canonical shepherd queue; sync to Postgres epic.priority (asc, lower=first). Operator edits priority via bees, never raw Postgres.

Source memories: feedback_bees_claude_teams_aware_format.md, feedback_bees_manager_serializes_writes.md, feedback_bees_ready_drives_picker.md
close_reason: extracted to /core:bees SKILL.md inline (3 subsections) in PR #78 (squash 764d30b); 2 archive + 1 root source memories deleted

title: skill-extract: git operations rules → /core:git-operations
description:
Extract from private memories into /core:git-operations SKILL.md.

Rules:
- Always use SSH-form git URLs (git@github.com:owner/repo.git) — never https for operations. SSH bypasses OAuth scope checks.
- Never use git worktrees for agent isolation — use shallow clone (--reference --dissociate) or cp -R. Worktrees share object DB + branch-lock = cross-session corruption.
- GH Releases on private repos return 404 (not 401) on anonymous access — always auth before fetching assets.
- GitHub auth is layered: gh keychain (primary) → scoped PATs (containers) → per-node OAuth. Don't use GITHUB_TOKEN env by default.  to add workflow scope.
- Git substrate preference: prefer git-backed designs (local/private/GitHub all work) for audit trail + replicability + merge semantics.

Source memories: feedback_git_ssh_only_for_operations.md, feedback_no_git_worktrees_for_agent_workspace.md, reference_gh_releases_private.md, feedback_github_auth_layered_no_static_creds.md, feedback_git_as_default_substrate.md
close_reason: extracted to /core:git SKILL.md inline (Remote and Authentication Conventions) in PR #78 (squash 764d30b); 5 archive source memories deleted

title: skill-extract: Phoenix binding + Logger + OpenAPI rules → /elixir:phoenix-framework and /elixir:style
description:
Extract from private memories into /elixir:phoenix-framework and /elixir:style.

Rules for /elixir:phoenix-framework:
- Phoenix Endpoint :ip MUST be at top level of runtime.exs, env-driven, unconditional — NOT gated inside an if-do block. Default 0.0.0.0. Never hardcode :: in prod-only block (IPv6-only = unreachable via Tailscale IPv4).
- PHX_HOST must match public DNS for LiveView websockets behind LB.
- Phoenix live reload doesn't catch lib/*.ex changes reliably — restart dev server after lib/, mix.exs, config/ changes. /api/info lies about in-memory code.
- OpenAPI contract-first: define spec before implementation, client built from spec.

Rules for /elixir:style:
- All Elixir output via Logger — never IO.puts/write. Flush concerns via Logger config + at_exit, not bypassing.
- No bang (!) functions in app code — use pattern matching for graceful errors.

Source memories: feedback_phx_bind_runtime_exs_unconditional.md, feedback_phx_host_loadbalancer.md, feedback_dev_server_restart_rule.md, feedback_openapi_contract_first.md, feedback_logger_only.md
close_reason: extracted to /elixir:phoenix SKILL.md (Runtime config, OpenAPI) + /elixir:style SKILL.md (Logger over IO.puts) in PR #78 (squash 764d30b); 5 archive source memories deleted

title: skill-extract: Elixir TDD rules (@cmd_mod, async, no integration tags) → /elixir:elixir-testing
description:
Extract from private memories into /elixir:elixir-testing SKILL.md.

Rules:
- @cmd_mod compile-time seam is the DEFAULT for every Elixir module that calls System.cmd/find_executable/Port.open. Pattern: @cmd_mod = Application.compile_env(:app, :cmd_module, System). Tests wire MockCmd via config/test.exs. Never naked System.cmd in lib/.
- ALL tests async: true — no exceptions without documented justification
- Mock ALL external calls (Claude CLI, Runex API, Linear API, BundleSync, git clone) — never probe live host
- Remove all @tag :integration / @moduletag :integration — no test exclusions by tag. Either mock the boundary or delete the test.
- There is no 'pre-existing' test failure — agents fix ALL CI failures before push regardless of origin
- No log noise in test output: config :logger, level: :none in test.exs
- All errors caught gracefully + logged via Logger.warning/error + return {:error, reason} — no else _ -> :ok

Source memories: feedback_cli_adapter_default_pattern.md, feedback_compile_time_external_seams.md (archived), feedback_test_async_mocking.md, feedback_remove_all_integration_labels_no_test_exclusions.md, feedback_no_preexisting_failures_mock_boundaries.md, feedback_no_silent_error_swallowing.md
close_reason: extracted to /elixir:testing references/elixir-tdd-discipline.md in PR #78 (squash 764d30b); 6 archive source memories deleted

title: skill-extract: Runex bundle structure + build workflow rules → /runex:runex
description:
Extract from private memories into /runex:runex SKILL.md.

Rules:
- Bundles: only dirs with workflow.toml under bundles/*/ are real bundles. Shared nu code goes in <repo>/lib/ or vendored into consumer's scripts/. Deploy artifacts go in <repo>/deploy/. workflow.name MUST match dir name. Every bundle has BUNDLE_VERSION in mise.toml. DAG via depends = [...] not needs.
- All bundle scripts must invoke tools via mise exec — never bare command names. npm-installed tools (claude, gh) whose wrapper re-invokes node must have node pinned in the same mise.toml.
- Build workflows must build in /tmp from fresh cache copy, never in operator's clone. Pattern: shallow-clone from  into /tmp/build/<id>/<app>, pull main, build, deploy, verify, cleanup.
- Always verify /api/info git_sha after deploy — not just /api/health. sha mismatch = stale BuildInfo.beam (rm it before mix release).
- Runex is CLI-first — runex server is one command; CLI works standalone with SQLite. Don't assume server is running.
- Runex defaults to SQLite; Postgres only via RUNEX_DATABASE_URL for federation.
- Tool resolution is per-node per-enabled-engine; don't shove all paths into PATH.
- Bundle publish must end with explicit POST /api/bundles/import to every registered target — never rely on BUNDLE_SOURCES auto-poll.
- No ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in workflow params — agents auth via local Claude Code, not API key.
- /api/runs response always wrapped under .data.

Source memories: feedback_bundle_structure.md, feedback_bundles_must_mise_exec.md, feedback_build_in_temp_dir_from_cache.md, feedback_build_always_pulls_latest_main.md, feedback_mix_release_buildinfo_stale.md, feedback_runex_cli_first.md, feedback_runex_default_sqlite.md, feedback_runex_tool_resolution_per_node_engine.md, feedback_always_explicit_bundle_import.md, feedback_no_anthropic_api_key_in_workflows.md, feedback_cache_isolation_from_user_clones.md, feedback_workspace_setup_cache_or_clone.md, feedback_validate_epic_runs_in_agent_workspace.md, feedback_runex_system_level.md
close_reason: extracted to /runex:runex references/bundle-build-rules.md in PR #78 (squash 764d30b); 14 archive source memories deleted

title: skill-extract: agent dispatch + delegation rules → /core:agent-loop
description:
Extract from multiple private memories into /core:agent-loop SKILL.md.

Rules:
- Every Agent dispatch must set model explicitly (haiku/sonnet/opus) — never inherit from parent (wastes tokens)
- Use specialized subagent_type (Explore, bees-manager, Plan, code-review-orchestrator) not always general-purpose
- Lead framing: I am Tier 1, spawned agents are Tier 2-5 (sub-team lead/worker/validator/fix). Never call sub-agents 'team lead'.
- Tier 1 lead does ZERO direct work — no Bash, Edit, Write, Read of source files. CI is always haiku. Fix is sonnet/opus.
- Never start a PR-opening agent prompt without explicit branch-from-fresh-main Step 0: git fetch origin main && git checkout origin/main && git checkout -b <branch>. Otherwise agent inherits working tree's current branch (often a sibling PR's stale branch).
- Spawned agents can't sustain timed polling loops (Monitor restricted, sleep blocked). Use one-shot snapshot agents + operator pings.
- Verify tool state via SSH/host inspection before claiming 'tool X missing on host Y' — often gap is agent PATH/env, not actual absence.
- Before any architectural proposal, search vantage_ex/architecture/decisions + /epics for existing ADRs.

Source memories: feedback_dispatch_model_and_subagent_discipline.md, feedback_agent_roles.md, feedback_team_lead_delegates_everything_including_ci.md, feedback_decompose_and_spawn.md, feedback_agent_skill_loading.md, feedback_agent_prompts_must_branch_from_fresh_main.md, feedback_no_haiku_polling_loops.md, feedback_verify_tool_state_via_ssh_not_agent_perspective.md, feedback_review_adrs_before_planning.md, feedback_haiku_behavior.md, feedback_worker_prompt_mirrors_autonomous_loop.md, feedback_session_start_compliance.md, feedback_mirror_existing_patterns_before_inventing.md

title: skill-extract: nushell Runex API + gotchas → /core:nushell
description:
Add Runex API response shape rules and common nushell gotchas to /core:nushell skill.

Rules from feedback_nu_chain_handoff_run_id_extraction.md:
- /api/runs responses always wrapped under .data — read data.<field> not <field>
- data.id is INT, must pipe | into string before string ops
- Terminal-status set must include 'success' (not just complete/failed/error/cancelled)
- Helpers must defensively unwrap: accept both {data:{...}} and {...} input forms

Rules from feedback_nu_gotchas.md:
- $env.HOME not $nu.home-path
- Parens in string interpolation parse as expressions — escape or lift to var
- sort-by -r not stable — sort then reverse
- No 2>&1 — use out+err>

Source memories: feedback_nu_chain_handoff_run_id_extraction.md + feedback_nu_gotchas.md

title: [task][P2] Add bees-manager agent to core plugin alongside /core:bees skill — global serial-writer for SQLite-single-writer constraint
description:
CRITICAL: Currently bees-manager exists as an operator-defined agent in the local Claude Code plugin/agents directory. Every workspace + repo that wants to use it must define it locally. We use bees-manager constantly in this workspace because of the SQLite single-writer constraint — concurrent workers that try to call `bees new`, `bees close`, `bees comment add`, or `bees dep add` on the same DB hit SQLITE_CONSTRAINT errors. The bees-manager pattern (lead spawns ONE bees-manager agent that applies all queued bees writes serially) is the canonical solution. It belongs in the marketplace alongside /core:bees so any team using bees gets it for free.

## Objective
Ship a `bees-manager` agent in the `vinnie357/claude-skills` plugin marketplace, paired with the existing `/core:bees` skill. The agent is loaded automatically when /core:bees is loaded and is invocable by lead agents to serialize bees writes.

## Context / Root Cause
Operator framing (verbatim 2026-05-07): "we should have a global one instead of one per repo".

Today's pattern (working but per-repo):
- Lead agent collects bees writes from worker reports (BEES REQUESTS: blocks)
- Lead spawns `bees-manager` (defined as a local Claude Code agent in operator's plugin/agents directory)
- bees-manager serializes the writes through the bees CLI (single-writer SQLite respected)
- No SQLITE_CONSTRAINT, no daemon.lock contention

The agent definition is custom. It should be marketplace-distributed.

Pairs with `feedback_bees_manager_serializes_writes.md` (operator memory documenting the pattern).

## Acceptance Criteria
- New agent at `~/github/claude-skills/plugins/core/agents/bees-manager.md` (verify path matches existing core agents layout — there may be `agents/` dir under each plugin)
- Agent frontmatter:
  - name: bees-manager
  - description: third person + "Use when there are queued bees writes (new/close/dep/update) — single-writer SQLite serialization avoids SQLITE_CONSTRAINT and daemon.lock failures from concurrent worker swarms."
  - tools: [Bash, Read, Grep] (read-only filesystem + bees CLI; no write tools beyond what bees CLI does)
- Agent body covers:
  - The SQLite single-writer constraint (cite /core:bees skill rule)
  - The "lead workers MUST NOT call write-mode bees commands directly" rule
  - The "lead emits BEES REQUESTS: block in worker final reports + the lead routes those to this agent" pattern
  - How to verify daemon.lock state, kill stale daemons, and recover from SQLITE_CONSTRAINT errors
  - Per-repo cwd handling (each bees DB is per-repo; bees-manager respects cwd to write the right DB)
  - Final-report shape (skill quotes + closures + new issues + dep wires + errors + lock state)
- Plugin manifest update: add the new agent to the core plugin's marketplace entry (`.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` or wherever the core plugin's agent list lives)
- Validation: run `nu test/validate-plugin.nu` (or canonical validator) — must pass
- /core:bees skill body adds a "Pairs with" reference to the bees-manager agent so users discover it

## Format requirements (per /claude-code:claude-agents skill)

- Frontmatter with name, description (third-person + "Use when..."), tools (allowlist NOT skills — agents have tools; skills don't have allowed-tools per the SessionStart contract for claude-code plugin)
- Body under 300 lines (overflow to references/)
- Zero hedging verbs — imperative
- References one level deep only

## Deliverables
- New agent at `plugins/core/agents/bees-manager.md`
- Updated marketplace manifest entry
- /core:bees skill body extended with "Pairs with bees-manager agent" reference
- Optional: a `references/sqlite-single-writer-recovery.md` if the recovery prose grows past the 300-line limit

Load skills: /claude-code:claude-agents /claude-code:claude-skills /core:bees /core:tdd /core:anti-fabrication /core:documentation-writing
close_reason: merged in https://github.com/vinnie357/claude-skills/pull/65

title: [task][P2] Create /elixir:ecto skill — Ecto schemas + changesets default, raw SQL is fallback
description:
CRITICAL: Implementer agents have twice written raw SQL where Ecto schemas + changesets are the right pattern. The first incident was direct epic writes (addressed by operator memory `feedback_dogfood_ecto_through_tidewave.md`); the second was an Ecto migration writing `repo().query!("UPDATE ...")`. Operator memories don't propagate to fresh sessions or other agents — a marketplace skill does. This skill becomes the canonical reference loaded by every Elixir worker.

## Objective
Create a new top-level skill `/elixir:ecto` in `vinnie357/claude-skills` plugin marketplace covering Ecto patterns: schemas, changesets, migrations, Repo API, query DSL, raw-SQL escape-hatch criteria. Discoverability: an agent doing data work loads `/elixir:ecto` directly without needing operator memory.

## Context / Root Cause
Existing Elixir skills in the marketplace cover:
- elixir:style (bang functions, pattern matching, idiomatic returns)
- elixir:elixir-testing (ExUnit, mocks)
- elixir:elixir-config (runtime vs compile-time)
- elixir:elixir-anti-patterns
- elixir:phoenix-framework
- elixir:tidewave

NONE of these emphasize the Ecto-vs-raw-SQL rule strongly enough for an implementer to internalize on first load. PR #268 (vantageex github-337 implementation) initially shipped a migration with `repo().query!("SELECT ...", [])` — operator caught + corrected. The skill prevents the next regression.

## Acceptance Criteria
The skill (`vinnie357/claude-skills/plugins/elixir/skills/ecto/SKILL.md`) covers MINIMUM:

1. **Default rule**: "Ecto schemas + changesets are the DEFAULT; raw SQL via Ecto.Adapters.SQL.query is the FALLBACK"
2. **Reads**: idiomatic Ecto query DSL (from, where, join, fragment), Repo.all/get/one
3. **Writes**: changeset construction, Repo.insert/update/delete, return tuple semantics ({:ok, _} | {:error, %Changeset{}})
4. **Batch writes**: Repo.update_all with set: keyword, when to prefer over per-row update
5. **Migrations**:
   - Schema changes via `add`, `alter`, `drop` macros
   - Data transformations via Ecto query DSL with LOCAL SNAPSHOT SCHEMA pattern (avoid importing the live application schema — locks migration to its-time state, future schema changes don't break replay)
   - When `execute/1` is appropriate (DB-specific DDL, raw indexes, extensions)
6. **Multi-step**: Ecto.Multi for atomic combos (insert + update + side effect with rollback semantics)
7. **When raw SQL IS appropriate** (escape-hatch criteria):
   - Perf-critical hot paths where Ecto-generated SQL is too slow (rare)
   - Complex CTEs / window functions Ecto doesn't expose
   - DB-specific features (PostGIS, extensions)
   - One-off ops in tidewave eval (single-cell read; never write)
8. **Anti-patterns to avoid**:
   - `repo().query!("SELECT ...", [])` in migrations (use Repo.all + local snapshot schema)
   - Bypassing changeset validation by direct insert_all/update_all when validation matters
   - Importing live application schema into old migrations (replay breakage)
   - Using raw SQL "because it's faster" without measurement
9. **Use when triggers** (in frontmatter description per claude-code:claude-skills convention):
   - "Use when writing Ecto migrations, querying or mutating Ecto schemas, designing changesets, or choosing between Ecto query DSL and raw SQL"
10. **Examples**: include 3-5 concrete code blocks per pattern (good vs bad pairs)

Format requirements (per /claude-code:claude-skills skill spec):
- Frontmatter with name, description (third person + "Use when..."), license
- NO `allowed-tools` field (per /claude-code:claude-skills validate-plugin.nu rule)
- Body under 300 lines (overflow to references/)
- Zero hedging verbs (no "should", "may", "might", "consider", "try to" — use imperative)
- References one level deep only

## Deliverables
- New skill at `~/github/claude-skills/plugins/elixir/skills/ecto/SKILL.md` (verify path matches existing elixir skills layout)
- references/ subdirectory if needed for examples that overflow 300 lines
- Plugin manifest update: add the new skill to the elixir plugin's marketplace entry (`.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` or wherever the elixir plugin's skill list lives)
- Validation: run `nu test/validate-plugin.nu` (or whatever the canonical validator is in claude-skills) — must pass
- A test fixture or CI assertion that the skill is discoverable by name

title: fix(security): gitleaks baseline not suppressing historical findings
description: Gitleaks reports 6 findings in git history at commit SHAs 6d6e3b79, 99b860b6, e08d4800, a33118eb in paths .gitleaks-baseline.json and skills/code-review/SKILL.md. The .gitleaks-baseline.json file exists but the current gitleaks invocation does not honor it properly, so these pre-existing historical findings are not being suppressed. Findings are pre-existing on main (verified: git branch --contains 6d6e3b79 includes main). The baseline file needs to be passed correctly to the gitleaks invocation, or the fingerprints need to be added/fixed in the baseline. Fingerprints from the 6 findings should be confirmed and added to .gitleaks-baseline.json to suppress them. Do NOT modify skills/code-review/SKILL.md content unless the secrets are real and must be removed from history.

title: claude-skills: extract feedback_no_todos_in_code into /core:code-review (Tier 5 scan) + /core:agent-loop (Tier 3 forbid)
description:
## Goal

Extract the "no TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK/DEFERRED comments in shipped code" rule from the operator's private memory `feedback_no_todos_in_code.md` into two claude-skills:

- `/core:code-review` — add a mandatory scan section (`git diff main..HEAD | grep -nE 'TODO|FIXME|XXX|HACK|DEFERRED'` returns empty)
- `/core:agent-loop` — add to Tier 3 implementer prompt template a "NO TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK/DEFERRED comments — implement now or escalate to lead for follow-up bee"

## Source memory

`/Users/vinnie/.claude/projects/-Users-vinnie-github/memory/feedback_no_todos_in_code.md`

## Acceptance criteria

- Both skills have the rule in their respective sections
- Marketplace.json version bumped if skill versioning is used
- After extraction, the private memory can be pruned in a follow-up
close_reason: duplicate of claude-skills-52

title: Migrate /core:mise skill from deprecated ubi backend to github backend
description: mise docs confirm the ubi backend is deprecated in favor of the github backend. The /core:mise SKILL.md has 22 references to ubi across the backends list, examples, and a full UBI Backend section (lines 217-265). Migrate all references to github:owner/repo syntax. ALSO: document that mise ls-remote works across backends for tool discovery (e.g., 'mise ls-remote github:juxt/allium-tools' to verify a backend returns versions before pinning) — this is a concrete anti-fabrication practice the skill should teach.
close_reason: merged in https://github.com/vinnie357/claude-skills/pull/62
acceptance_criteria: Zero 'ubi' mentions remain in plugins/core/skills/mise/ except in a brief deprecation/migration note. Replacements use github:owner/repo syntax verified via mise ls-remote for sharkdp/fd, BurntSushi/ripgrep, goreleaser/goreleaser, cli/cli. Skill documents the mise ls-remote discovery pattern with at least one concrete backend example per supported backend (github, cargo, npm, go). mise test and mise test:plugin core pass. core plugin and all-skills patch-bumped. PR opened and CI green.

title: Update runex skill API template with bundle endpoints
description:
The API template at plugins/tools/runex/skills/runex/templates/0.1.0/api.md documents GET /api/health, GET /api/workflows, GET/POST /api/runs, etc. but is missing:

- POST /api/bundles/import (multipart tar.gz upload)
- POST /api/bundles/sync (trigger bundle sync)
- POST /api/bundles/webhook
- POST /api/workflows/import (content/URL/git source)
- GET /api/info
- GET /api/runs/:id/steps/:step_id

Add request/response shapes for each based on the actual Runex router and controllers.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #52
acceptance_criteria:
- API template covers all current Runex endpoints
- Request/response shapes match actual controller behavior
